maml:description.maml:para: Resolves DNS records (A, AAAA, MX, TXT, …) over UDP/TCP/DoT/DoH with optional multi-resolver strategies.

Supports single-provider queries, multiple providers with FirstSuccess/FastestWins/SequentialAll/RoundRobin, direct endpoints (IPv4/IPv6/DoH URLs), concurrency control, and TTL-based response caching.
The name of the DNS record to query for
Pattern to expand into multiple DNS queries.

Predefined provider(s) (DnsEndpoint) for the query. When a single provider is specified, the classic single-resolver path is used. When multiple providers are specified, the multi-resolver path is used. If not specified, the default provider System (UDP) is used.

Possible values: System, SystemTcp, Cloudflare, CloudflareSecurity, CloudflareFamily, CloudflareWireFormat, CloudflareWireFormatPost, CloudflareJsonPost, Google, GoogleWireFormat, GoogleWireFormatPost, GoogleJsonPost, Quad9, Quad9ECS, Quad9Unsecure, OpenDNS, OpenDNSFamily, CloudflareQuic, GoogleQuic, AdGuard, AdGuardFamily, AdGuardNonFiltering, NextDNS, DnsCryptCloudflare, DnsCryptQuad9, DnsCryptRelay, RootServer, CloudflareOdoh, Custom
One or more resolver endpoints in string format. Accepted: "1.1.1.1:53", "[2606:4700:4700::1111]:53", "dns.google:53", or DoH URLs like "https://dns.google/dns-query".

One or more predefined providers (DnsEndpoint enum) to expand into endpoints for the multi-resolver. This enables strategy control (FirstSuccess/FastestWins/SequentialAll) and other multi-resolver options.

Possible values: System, SystemTcp, Cloudflare, CloudflareSecurity, CloudflareFamily, CloudflareWireFormat, CloudflareWireFormatPost, CloudflareJsonPost, Google, GoogleWireFormat, GoogleWireFormatPost, GoogleJsonPost, Quad9, Quad9ECS, Quad9Unsecure, OpenDNS, OpenDNSFamily, CloudflareQuic, GoogleQuic, AdGuard, AdGuardFamily, AdGuardNonFiltering, NextDNS, DnsCryptCloudflare, DnsCryptQuad9, DnsCryptRelay, RootServer, CloudflareOdoh, Custom
Multi-resolver strategy to use when multiple endpoints are provided.
Possible values: FirstSuccess, FastestWins, SequentialAll, RoundRobin
Limits concurrent queries across endpoints. Defaults to 4.
Respect endpoint-level timeouts if present. When not set, the cmdlet's -TimeOut value is used.
Cache duration in minutes for FastestWins strategy.
Limits concurrent queries per endpoint when using the multi-resolver. Set to a positive value to cap in-flight queries per endpoint; 0 disables the cap.
Enables response caching based on TTLs for repeated queries of the same (name,type). Disabled by default.
Optional override for default cache expiration when TTL is unavailable (seconds).
Minimal TTL allowed for cached entries (seconds). 0 leaves library default.
Maximal TTL allowed for cached entries (seconds). 0 leaves library default.
The type of the record to query for. If not specified, A record is queried.
Server to use for the query. If not specified, the default provider System (UDP) is used. Once a server is specified, the query will be sent to that server.
If specified, all servers listed in Server are queried sequentially and the responses are aggregated in server order. When not specified, only the first server is queried for faster results.
If specified, the cmdlet sequentially queries each server until a successful response is received. This option stops on the first server that returns DnsResponseCode.NoError.
If specified, the order of servers defined in Server is randomized before querying.

Provides the full response of the query. If not specified, only the minimal response is provided (just the answer). If specified, the full response is provided (answer, authority, and additional sections).
When set, attempts to parse answers into typed record objects.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether TXT records should be parsed into specialized types (DMARC, SPF, etc.) when TypedRecords is specified. When false, returns simple TXT records.

Specifies the timeout for the DNS query, in milliseconds. If the DNS server does not respond within this time, the query will fail. Default is 2000 ms (2 seconds) as defined by DefaultTimeout. Increase this value for slow networks or unreliable servers.
Number of retry attempts on transient errors.
Delay between retry attempts in milliseconds.
Request DNSSEC data (sets the DO bit).
Validate DNSSEC signatures. Implies requesting DNSSEC data.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enable TTL-based response cache with bounds

maml:description.maml:para: Clears the multi-resolver fastest-endpoint cache used by the FastestWins strategy.

Clears the in-memory cache used to remember the fastest endpoint for a given set of endpoints. Use this when you change network conditions or want to force re-probing. Does not affect TTL-based response caching, which expires automatically.
Clear all cached fastest-endpoint choices.
Specific endpoints to clear, e.g. "1.1.1.1:53", "[2606:4700:4700::1111]:53", or DoH URLs.
Clear cache entries for the specified predefined providers (DnsEndpoint enum).

Possible values: System, SystemTcp, Cloudflare, CloudflareSecurity, CloudflareFamily, CloudflareWireFormat, CloudflareWireFormatPost, CloudflareJsonPost, Google, GoogleWireFormat, GoogleWireFormatPost, GoogleJsonPost, Quad9, Quad9ECS, Quad9Unsecure, OpenDNS, OpenDNSFamily, CloudflareQuic, GoogleQuic, AdGuard, AdGuardFamily, AdGuardNonFiltering, NextDNS, DnsCryptCloudflare, DnsCryptQuad9, DnsCryptRelay, RootServer, CloudflareOdoh, Custom
Alternate parameter: providers to clear when using the classic -DnsProvider parameter style.

Possible values: System, SystemTcp, Cloudflare, CloudflareSecurity, CloudflareFamily, CloudflareWireFormat, CloudflareWireFormatPost, CloudflareJsonPost, Google, GoogleWireFormat, GoogleWireFormatPost, GoogleJsonPost, Quad9, Quad9ECS, Quad9Unsecure, OpenDNS, OpenDNSFamily, CloudflareQuic, GoogleQuic, AdGuard, AdGuardFamily, AdGuardNonFiltering, NextDNS, DnsCryptCloudflare, DnsCryptQuad9, DnsCryptRelay, RootServer, CloudflareOdoh, Custom

dev:remarks.maml:para: Clears cache entries only for the specified provider set.
Clears cache entries only for the specified endpoints.

maml:description.maml:para: Discovers services advertised via DNS Service Discovery.
Wraps CancellationToken) to return services under a domain.
Domain name to search for advertised services.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Filter for a specific service type

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves services advertised via DNS Service Discovery.
Queries the _services._dns-sd._udp tree for the specified domain and returns SRV/TXT data describing each advertised service.
Domain name to search for advertised services.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Pipe results to select useful fields

maml:description.maml:para: Sends DNS UPDATE messages to a server.
Adds or removes records in a zone using RFC 2136 over TCP.
Zone to update.
DNS server to send the update to.
Port number to use. Defaults to 53.
Record name.
Type of record.

Possible values: Reserved, A, NS, MD, MF, CNAME, SOA, MB, MG, MR, NULL, WKS, PTR, HINFO, MINFO, MX, TXT, RP, AFSDB, X25, ISDN, RT, NSAP, NSAP_PTR, SIG, AAAA, LOC, SRV, ATMA, NAPTR, KX, CERT, A6, DNAME, SINK, OPT, APL, DS, SSHFP, IPSECKEY, RRSIG, NSEC, DNSKEY, DHCID, NSEC3, NSEC3PARAM, TLSA, SMIMEA, HIP, NINFO, RKEY, TALINK, CDS, CDNSKEY, OPENPGPKEY, CSYNC, ZONEMD, SVCB, HTTPS, TKEY, TSIG, IXFR, AXFR, MAILB, MAILA, ANY, SPF, URI, CAA, AVC, DOA, AMTRELAY, RESINFO, TA, DLV
Record data used when adding a record.
TTL for the new record. Defaults to 300 seconds.
If specified, the record is removed instead of added.
Performs a DNS zone transfer (AXFR) for a given zone.
Retrieves all records for the specified zone using TCP based zone transfer.
The zone to transfer.
DNS server to query.
Port number to use. Defaults to 53.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Transfer a zone from a custom port
